en.extract:
Forbes-Watson's swift (Apus berliozi) is a species of swift in the family Apodidae.
It breeds in coastal areas of Somalia and the southern Arabian Peninsula and on the island of Socotra. In the non-breeding season it has been observed as far south as coastal Mozambique.

'''Forbes-Watson's swift''' ('''''Apus berliozi''''') is a species of [[Swift (bird)|swift]] in the family [[Apodidae]].
It breeds in coastal areas of [[Somalia]] and the southern [[Arabian Peninsula]] and on the island of [[Socotra]]. In the non-breeding season it has been observed as far south as coastal Mozambique.<ref>{{cite journal |last1=Marais |first1=Etienne |last2=Peacock |first2=Faansie |last3=Allport |first3=Gary |title=First record of Forbes-Watson's Swift Apus berliozi in southern Africa, with comments on vocal and visual identification |journal=Bulletin of the British Ornithologists' Club |date=9 March 2021 |volume=141 |issue=1 |pages=21–38 |doi=10.25226/bboc.v141i1.2021.a3 |doi-access=free }}</ref>
